pages.text:
MLS NEXT HOMEGROWN DIVISION
RULES & REGULATIONS
2025-2026

I. OVERVIEW
Player Development, L.L.C. (“PDev”), a subsidiary of Major League Soccer, L.L.C. (“MLS”),
organizes, manages, and administers an elite youth development platform (“Program” or “League”
operating as “MLS NEXT Homegrown Division”) with the mission of providing world class
training, development, education, and competition opportunities for select soccer players between
the age groups of U13 to U19 across the United States and Canada. MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division also provides a professional player development and an elite development pathway for
future college, professional, and international players. The Program includes a league, postseason
competition, as well as other development-focused initiatives.
II. DEFINITIONS
Capitalized terms shall have the definitions ascribed to them in this section. Capitalized terms not
defined in this section but defined in other sections of these Rules and Regulations or in other
Rules and Policies (as defined below) will have the meaning ascribed to them in those sections or
documents.
a. Club (or “Member”). Any soccer club whose application for membership in MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division has been accepted, and that has signed a Membership Agreement
and paid a performance bond in the amount directed by PDev.
b. Club Representative. Any Club personnel, including but not limited to, any Club
employees, Coaches, trainers, Team administrators, other support or volunteer staff, or
any individual who represents themselves as being affiliated with a Club in a manner that
is known or should be known to the Club, that assist the Club in connection with the
Season.
c. Coach. An individual who performs the duties and services as may be assigned to an
individual by a Club regarding the training of youth soccer players, including, but not
limited to, (i) conducting and supervising the Team’s practices, training camps, and
training sessions, (ii) coaching the Team in all competitions and Matches, (iii) preparing
for all Matches played by the Team, including developing Match strategies, (iv)
overseeing off-season Player development programs for the Team, (v) consulting and
advising with respect to the Team’s Roster and Player trades, (vi) directing and
supervising the Team’s coaching staff, (vii) enforcing Club and League rules, and/or
(viii) participating in promotional, sponsorship, and public relations activities.
d. Competitive Season. The period of time between the first official Match scheduled by
PDev and the final official Match scheduled by PDev (including MLS NEXT Cup).
e. Conference. The grouping of Teams that compete against each other in the Regular
Season and whose performance and standings are tracked and reported together.
f. CSA. The Canadian Soccer Association, the governing body for soccer in Canada.
1

g. Facility. A venue for Team training and Matches, including all parking, structures, and
grounds on or immediately surrounding the venue.
h. FIFA. The Fédération Internationale de Football Association (International Federation of
Association Football), the international governing body of association soccer.
i. Fixture. A set of scheduled Matches of multiple age groups versus one opponent.
j. Forfeit. A 3-0 loss for the team that is issued the forfeiture for a Match.
k. Future Players: A Player who is Rostered with a Club who is not ready to contribute at
the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division level full time.
l. Infraction. Any violation or alleged violation of the Rules and Policies, or any action or
alleged action that is against the spirit of the Rules and Policies.
m. Lifetime Ban. Permanently prohibiting an individual from being a Participant.
n. Match/Game. Any soccer match played between two Clubs, or any match organized by
PDev, as part of an MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competition.
o. Match Day Roster. The official match day roster that each Team must create and submit
to the referee prior to a Match, which for U13 through U19 lists 18 Players from the
Roster who will be eligible to participate in that Match as well as all individuals who will
be in the Club’s technical area at any point during the Match.
p. Match Decision. Events or matters that have a direct effect on Matches, excluding
disciplinary matters, including, but not limited to, the eligibility of Players, the state of
the field, accessory Match equipment, crowd behavior, and stadium installations.
q. Match Official. The referee, assistant referees, fourth official, match commissioner,
referee inspector, the person in charge of safety, and/or any other persons appointed by
PDev and/or Professional Referee Organization (“PRO”) to assume responsibility in
connection with a Match.
r. Match Report. A form submitted by a Match Official that records Match data on the field
of play, including, but not limited to, a win, loss, tie, goals scored for, goals scored
against, total points achieved, misconduct, suspensions, and injuries.
s. Match Suspension. A ban that prevents a Player, Club Representative (in the technical
area), or other Participant from taking part in any future Match or MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Competition, including, but not limited to, being present in the area
immediately surrounding the field of play. The suspension is imposed in terms of
Matches, days, or months.
2

t. Membership Agreement. The document that details the rights and obligations of each
Club; a fully executed Membership Agreement is required for a Club to become a valid
member of MLS NEXT Homegrown Division.
u. MLS Academy. An institution that focuses on youth player development and is owned
and operated by a Club that competes in Major League Soccer.
v. MLS NEXT Cup. The MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competition held at the
conclusion of the Regular Season among the qualifying Teams from the U15 through
U19 age groups, culminating in a champion in the U15, U16, U17, and U19 age groups.
w. MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competition. The MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
schedule, collectively referring to friendly matches, Regular Season matches, MLS
NEXT Flex, Generation adidas Cup, MLS NEXT Fest, MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division Showcases, All-Star Games, and MLS NEXT Cup (each as applicable),
featuring at least one Club and organized by PDev.
x. MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Events. This definition includes, but is not limited to,
MLS NEXT Homegrown Division-sanctioned- tournaments, leagues, showcases, Talent
IDs, and camps run by PDev or Clubs, including local affiliate organizations (if the event
is sanctioned by PDev). While PDev may not have authority or control over non-
sanctioned events operated or organized by Clubs, any conduct prohibited by the Rules
and Policies even at non-sanctioned events may serve as an independent basis for
disciplinary action if it calls into question a Participant’s fitness to participate in MLS
NEXT Homegrown Division Events or MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competitions.
y. MLS NEXT Homegrown Division High School Waiver. The waiver form that must be
completed if a registered Primary Player’s admission or scholarship at a high school
requires the Player’s participation on the school’s high school soccer team.
z. MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Participant Agreement and Waiver. The waiver and
release agreement that all Players (and, to the extent applicable, Players’ parents/legal
guardians), Club Representatives, or any other Participant must sign before they may
participate in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division.
aa. MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Showcases. MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
Competitions held nationally by PDev across select age groups. Unless otherwise stated
by MLS NEXT, Club participation is optional at MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
Showcases. MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Showcases are part of the Regular
Season.
bb. MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Technical Standards. All rules and guidelines related
to the technical soccer standards of any MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competition
as well as training standards. These include, but are not limited to, substitutions, field
size, and coach licensing and certification.
3

cc. MLS NEXT Website. The official MLS NEXT website, currently
www.mlssoccer.com/mlsnext.
dd. Participant. Any Player, Club, Club Representative, PDev employee that does not
otherwise fall under the definition of Program Staff, Match Official, parent, spectator,
volunteer, independent contractor (including, but not limited to, security or medical
personnel including an athletic trainer that does not fall under Club Representative), or
other individual that attends or contributes to an MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
Event.
ee. Partnership. A contractual relationship between an MLS Club and a non-MLS Club,
where each agree to cooperate and advance their mutual interests. To the extent the
contractual relationship includes the right to use or license MLS Club trademarks, service
marks, trade names, trade dress, brands, insignia, symbols, logos, crests, decorative
designs, and any other similar marks (collectively, the “Club Marks”), the agreement
must be signed and approved by PDev.
ff. Player. An individual who has completed the Registration process and is eligible to be
added to a Roster.
gg. Player Add. The number of Players added to a Club between August 22, 2025, through
April 16, 2026; Clubs may add a Player as a new Primary Player, transfer a Player to
another Club, or add a Future Player as a Primary Player.
hh. Pre-Season. The three-to-four week period before the start of the MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Regular Season.
ii. Post-Season. The MLS NEXT Cup.
jj. Primary Player. Top players at each Club who are expected to be registered only with
MLS NEXT Homegrown Division and cannot play in any outside competition or league
without prior approval by PDev.
kk. Program Staff. Those individuals who work on behalf of PDev to execute all MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Events.
ll. Protest. An objection of any kind related to a Match Decision.
mm. Registration. The process by which each Player, Club, and Club Representative submits
required information and agrees to abide by the Rules and Policies. Registration is
currently done online.
nn. Regular Season. The time period beginning on a date set by PDev (typically in
September) and concluding on a date specified by PDev before the beginning of the MLS
4

NEXT Cup during which MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competition between Clubs
occurs, inclusive of regional and winter MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Showcases,
and counting towards points for regular season standings.
oo. Roster. The list of Players and Club Representatives registered to participate on a Team.
pp. Roster Freeze. The deadline to make any roster adjustments during the Season.
qq. Rules and Policies. Collectively, these MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Rules and
Regulations, along with each Club’s Membership Agreement, the MLS NEXT
Commercial Marketing Guidelines, the MLS NEXT Style Guide, the MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Disciplinary Code, the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Code of
Conduct, the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy, the MLS
Privacy Policy, and any other rules, regulations, policies, guidelines, procedures, or
directives of PDev, including, without limitation, any other organizational agreements of
PDev and its affiliates (in each case, as they exist on the date hereof and as they may be
amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time by PDev).
rr. Sanction. A penalty or other means to enforce compliance with Rules and Policies.
ss. Season. The entire year of competitive and non-competitive programming organized by
PDev as MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Events, including, but not limited to, Pre-
Season, Regular Season, Post-Season, and any other events organized by PDev.
tt. Semester. The MLS NEXT Homegrown Division periods constituting: (i) Fall (August
through and including MLS NEXT Fest); and (ii) Spring (conclusion of MLS NEXT Fest
through and including MLS Next Cup).
uu. Team. Any grouping of Players by age by a Club to play in MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division Competitions.
vv. USSF (the “Federation” or “U.S. Soccer”). The United States Soccer Federation, the
official governing body of the sport of soccer in the United States.
5

III. SAFESPORT, ANTI-ABUSE, ANTI-DISCRIMINATION, AND SAFETY
COMPLIANCE FRAMEWORK
PDev is committed to making soccer the preeminent sport in the United States by serving our
Players, Members, and their fans. The MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing
Policy, which incorporates policies mandated by federal law, the U.S. Center for SafeSport (the
“Center”), and Canada Soccer’s equivalent SafeSport policy (as promulgated by the Canadian
Centre for Ethics in Sport (“CCES”), Sport Information Resource Centre (“SIRC”), and by Sport
Canada), as well as the U.S. Soccer Federation, represents a foundational structure for building a
soccer community where all participants, be they athletes, coaches, referees, parents,
administrators, or volunteers, play an active role in creating a soccer atmosphere free of all forms
of emotional, physical, and sexual misconduct.
In addition, the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy prohibits engaging
in any conduct that amounts to unfair, adverse, discriminatory, or unequal treatment based on an
individual’s Protected Status, including, without limitation, racism, or sexism. PDev is committed
to maintaining an environment at all MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Events that is free from
all forms of discrimination.
The MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy is incorporated into all Rules
and Policies and can be found on the MLS NEXT Website. Failure of any Participant to comply
with the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy or the MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Code of Conduct will subject the non-compliant Club and/or Participant to
discipline in accordance with the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Disciplinary Code and the
MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy.
IV. GOVERNANCE AND ORGANIZATION
a. PDev’s Authority & Discretion
MLS NEXT Homegrown Division is organized, managed, and administered by PDev. By signing
the Membership Agreement, Clubs agree that the decision-making and policy-setting of PDev,
including, but not limited to, setting forth the Rules and Policies, setting a competition schedule,
and imposing a framework for issuing discipline, shall be made by PDev in its sole and absolute
discretion.
b. Discipline
Any and all Infractions may subject a Club or a Participant to discipline under the MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Disciplinary Code. Should a Club or Participant be found in violation of any
of the Rules and Policies, penalties may vary based on the type and frequency of the Infraction
and may result in fines, denied participation in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competitions
and MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Events, expulsion from the Program, among other potential
penalties in PDev’s sole and absolute discretion. The Clubs may bear the financial costs of any
training mandated as a result of any rule violation. For more information, please see the MLS
NEXT Homegrown Division Disciplinary Code.
6

c. Committee Structure
PDev believes that the input, collaboration, and expertise of its Clubs will enhance its success.
Therefore, PDev will create the Membership Advisory Committee (“MAC”), an ad hoc committee
to receive input from its members in areas such as: competition, environment, individual growth,
Player identification, commercial opportunities, and equity and inclusion. The MAC provides a
forum for Participants to communicate their opinions, share their expertise, study issues,
coordinate services, and develop recommendations. PDev will retain final decision-making
authority but will consider recommendations presented by the MAC.
PDev may create additional Working Groups during the course of the 2025–2026 Season, or future
seasons, to ensure engagement and collaboration with key soccer stakeholders in the U.S. and
Canada.
This section sets out the responsibilities, objectives, authority, responsibilities, composition, and
operation of the MAC:
1. MAC Objectives
The MAC shall be responsible for the following:
• Facilitate collaboration, cooperation, and understanding between PDev and Participants so
that PDev may react faster to Participants’ interests, priorities, and concerns.
• The MAC will provide a forum for Participants to communicate their opinions, share their
expertise, study issues, coordinate services, and develop recommendations. PDev shall
rotate MAC members every two years.
2. MAC Membership
The MLS NEXT General Manager is the MAC Chairman. The Chairman will work with and
receive nominations from Participants for membership on the MAC. The Chairman shall have sole
and absolute authority to appoint members to the MAC. The MAC shall consist of at least twelve
(12) members, comprising of both MLS Academy and non-MLS Academy Club representation.
The membership of the MAC shall rotate every two years.
3. MAC Meetings
The MAC shall meet with such frequency and at such intervals as the Chairman determines is
necessary to carry out the MAC’s duties and responsibilities but will plan to meet on a quarterly
cadence. The MAC may permit attendance at meetings by Club management or other members as
the MAC determines appropriate or advisable from time to time.
7

4. MAC Goals
The MAC shall establish goals for the following year at the last annual meeting. The MAC shall
present its written goals to all Participants on an annual basis. In addition, at the end of each two-
year cycle, the MAC shall present a written report detailing the MAC’s actions in satisfaction of
its stated goals and objectives.
V. MLS NEXT HOMEGROWN DIVISION MEMBERSHIP
a. Consideration For Membership
Each Club that wishes to participate in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division must submit a
completed application prior to the application deadline determined by PDev to be considered for
admission into the Program. The precise criteria that will be applied in assessing applicant clubs
will be determined by PDev in its sole and absolute discretion. However, it is expected that the
following factors, among others, may be considered, with the weight of each factor to be decided
by PDev in its sole and absolute discretion:
i. Club philosophy
ii. Leadership and governance
iii. History of elite player development
iv. Strength of current and potential player pool
v. Expertise and credentials of coaches and technical staff
vi. Daily performance environment, including training, matches, facilities,
staff support, non-soccer programs, etc.
vii. Ability to reduce or eliminate costs for players
viii. Geography
ix. Disciplinary history (including criminal or Safety and Wellbeing violations)
x. Evidence of non-discriminatory, inclusive policies, processes and practices
The Program Staff will aim to review and evaluate each application every calendar year. Once the
Program Staff has reviewed the submitted applications, the Program Staff will conduct live
(virtual) interviews with clubs under consideration and gather feedback from local and national
references.
PDev will make the final determination regarding which clubs are accepted and offered the
opportunity to become Members of MLS NEXT Homegrown Division. An accepted Club will not
become a Member until the Club has agreed to abide by all Rules and Policies, provided the
8

required performance-bond payment to PDev, and entered into a fully-executed Membership
Agreement.
b. Maintaining Eligibility
To remain in good standing, Clubs must be in full compliance with the Rules and Policies. In
addition, PDev reserves the right to conduct evaluations of Clubs at any time; such evaluations
may include review of both technical and administrative criteria (including as set forth above in
Section V(a)), as determined by PDev in its sole discretion, as well as a review of the Club’s
compliance with the Rules and Policies.
c. Change of Ownership
A Club must give PDev written notice if the Club wants to change ownership, merge with another
Club, change the location of the Club, or change the structure of the Club. PDev reserves the right
to deny the Club’s request, conduct a review of the Club prior to any such change, or determine
whether the Club will remain in good standing. If PDev determines that a Club is not in compliance
with such criteria or any of the Rules and Policies, the Club may be subject to discipline, up to and
including removal from MLS NEXT Homegrown Division at PDev’s sole discretion.
d. Withdrawal of a Club
In the event a Club withdraws from MLS NEXT Homegrown Division without finishing its
scheduled Season, the Club shall forfeit its Membership and performance bond, and the following
will apply:
i. If at least half of the full schedule of Matches for the Club in question has
been played, all resulting scores stand as validated by PDev;
ii. If less than half of the full schedule of Matches for the Club in question has
been played, these completed Matches will be deleted from the standings;
and
iii. In the sole and absolute discretion of PDev, any remaining Matches shall
either be recorded as a Forfeit victory for the opponent and/or rescheduled,
as may be appropriate.
e. Termination
Pursuant to the Membership Agreement, PDev reserves the right to terminate any Club’s
Membership at any time for failure to meet MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Technical
Standards, violation of any of the Rules and Policies, or taking or failing to stop any action that
frustrates the purpose or intent of any Rules and Policies, in each case such determination shall be
in accordance with the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Disciplinary Code.
9

VI. CLUB NON-FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITIES
a. Compliance with Rules and Policies
All Clubs and Participants are required to comply with the Rules and Policies. In order to comply,
Clubs must adopt the Rules and Policies on behalf of their employees and all other Club
Representatives. PDev reserves the right to decide how to enforce the Rules and Policies, in its
sole and absolute discretion. Should a Club or any Participant associated with that Club be found
in violation of any of the Rules and Policies, the Club’s standing and Membership in MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division, or the individual’s participation in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division, may
be jeopardized, as further described below. In addition, the Rules and Policies are subject to change
from time to time. Although PDev makes every effort not to modify the Rules and Policies mid-
season, PDev reserves the right, in its sole and absolute discretion, to amend or enact new policies
at any time, particularly to the extent required by applicable law, recommended by the U.S.
Olympic and Paralympic Committee or the Center, or otherwise where PDev believes it is in the
best interest of MLS NEXT Homegrown Division and/or the safety of its Players. In the event of
a change of these Rules and Policies, PDev will make best efforts to provide Clubs with advance
notice.
Subject to any applicable laws, PDev may use any information in determining disqualifications for
participation in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division in PDev’s absolute discretion. Such
information may include, without limitation, conviction of any felony, gross misdemeanors,
violation of Rules and Policies, or driving offenses, including, but not limited to: loss of license,
reckless driving, impaired driving, or a DUI.
Any person accused of physical or sexual misconduct or otherwise violating the MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy may be terminated, suspended, or placed in an
alternate position by PDev until the matter is resolved. A person who is accused, but later cleared,
of charges/allegations may apply to be reinstated in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division, which
decision shall be in PDev’s sole and absolute discretion. Reinstatement is not a right, and no
guarantee is made that they will be reinstated to their former position or any other position within
the MLS NEXT ecosystem.
b. Reporting Obligations
All Clubs and Covered Persons (as defined in the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and
Wellbeing Policy to mean any individual age eighteen or older (“Adult”) that is a Participant who
has Regular Contact (which means, recurring, repeated or periodic contact between an Adult and
anyone under the lesser of (1) the age of 18, or (2) the age of majority in the applicable state or
province (“Minor”), or a person with supervisory or decision-making authority over an Adult who
has recurring, repeated or periodic contact with Minors, including but not limited to overseeing,
supervising, chaperoning, or otherwise interacting) with any Player or Match Official, who is a
Minor, in connection with Covered Programs (as defined in the Safety and Wellbeing Policy))
must comply with all reporting obligations set forth in the Rules and Policies. Failure by Clubs or
Covered Persons to promptly report violations of the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety
and Wellbeing Policy and SafeSport Code constitutes a violation of the Rules and Policies. Clubs,
10

Covered Persons, and all Participants should report pertinent situations that appear to be in non-
compliance with the Rules and Policies to PDev. Reporting non-compliance on the part of other
Clubs should not be viewed as a negative, but rather as a positive attempt to maintain consistent
standards that ensure professionalism, safety, and fairness, both on and off the field.
c. Conduct
Each Club must, upon PDev’s renewal of a Club’s membership in MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division, execute and deliver its annual Membership Agreement to PDev prior to the start of the
upcoming season. In the event a Club does not renew its Membership by executing the annual
Membership Agreement and paying the performance bond as directed by PDev, such Club will
not be permitted to participate in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Events for that year. In the
event a Club does not provide adequate notice (at least thirty (30) days) of such non-renewal, the
Club will forfeit its performance bond. Each Club shall be responsible for the conduct of all its
Participants, including, but not limited to, its Club Representatives, Players, parents, volunteers,
and spectators.
d. Development
i. Heading:
1. Players aged 10 and younger are prohibited from heading the ball
regardless of the age group in which they play.
2. Players who are 11, 12, and 13, regardless of the age group in which
they play, may head the ball during Matches; however, these players
must limit heading practice to a maximum of 30 minutes per week
with no more than 15–20 headers per player, per week.
e. Scholarships
i. Requirements:
1. Each Club in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division is required to issue
a minimum of one (1) full scholarship equivalent per season.
2. The full scholarship equivalent must be a financial amount equal to
the cost of participating on an MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
team, excluding travel (i.e., cost of tuition).
a. The financial support that results in a full scholarship
equivalent may be issued across multiple families (e.g.,
instead of providing a single player/family one (1) full
scholarship, a Club may issue financial assistance to four (4)
families that is equal to 25% of a full scholarship).
11

ii. Recommendations (not required):
1. Club financial support to families is “need-based.”
2. Clubs issue financial support across several age groups.
VII. OUTSIDE COMPETITION
a. Permissible Outside Competition
Subject to approval by PDev (and in the event of international tournament participation, additional
PDev, USSF, and/or Canada Soccer approvals may be required), Clubs may participate in elite
tournaments outside of the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Program. Clubs will only be
permitted to participate in approved competitions and events that follow the MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Technical Standards and are in compliance with the standards outlined in
the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Outside Competition Standards (available in the Outside
Competition Form). Clubs must receive prior approval from PDev for all elite tournaments outside
of the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Program each Season. Club participation in outside
competition without prior approval may result in discipline.
To request permission, the Club must submit to PDev an MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
Outside Competition Application, including a roster and all necessary background information, no
later than eight (8) weeks before the event. In the event the Team plans to travel internationally,
the Club must also submit the appropriate documentation to the USSF (if the Club is based in the
U.S.) or CSA (if the Club is based in Canada).
The MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy and the prevention policies
therein remain applicable to any Club traveling internationally.
b. Player Participation in Outside Competitions
Players must meet all attendance and participation requirements under the Rules and Policies and
under any individual Club requirements with respect to training and Matches for their Club during
the Season. Absent extenuating circumstances (to be determined by PDev and the Club), the failure
to meet those requirements should result in the Player’s removal from the Club. PDev strongly
encourages Clubs to ensure that Players meet the attendance and participation requirements—
Clubs that do not take appropriate action to ensure that such attendance and participation
requirements are met may be subject to Sanctions.
Clubs may not permit any Primary Player to participate in non-MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
soccer activities during the Season, except as set forth in this Section and in connection with a
Player’s National Team duty and/or Youth National Team Identification Center participation.
Clubs will be held accountable for the actions of their Players and Club Representatives, as well
as the Club’s parents, spectators, and volunteers. Players will be subject to discipline by both their
own Club and PDev.
12

Future Players are able to participate in outside soccer leagues, as long as they are participating
with their Club. Future Players cannot participate in outside soccer leagues with any other club.
c. Player Participation in Middle and High School Soccer
Primary Players must agree to forgo participating simultaneously in both (1) MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division and (2) high school or middle school soccer.
Except as set forth herein, Primary Players who participate in high/middle school soccer are
ineligible to participate in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Events.
Only Primary Players who have been granted a High School Waiver by PDev will be allowed to
remain on their Club’s Roster during the high school soccer season (waivers are not available for
middle school). Those Primary Players are permitted to participate in both high school soccer and
MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competition simultaneously during this period.
This waiver must be granted by PDev during one of the High School Waiver Windows to be
considered for the Roster exemption for the 2025–2026 MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Season.
The waiver eligibility guidelines are outlined in the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division High
School Waiver. The High School Waiver Windows are set forth below:
i. Fall and Winter high school soccer season participants must submit the
High School Waiver no later than September 9, 2025.
ii. Spring high school soccer season participants must submit the High School
Waiver between January 1, 2026, and February 13, 2026.
Although Clubs are not limited to a specific number of waivers, the number is naturally self-
limiting to field a full Team throughout the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Season.
If a waiver is granted, the Primary Player must comply with the disclosure requirement and secure
a full injury and disciplinary report from their high school team to provide to their Club.
All Primary Players who do not meet waiver eligibility and choose to participate with their high
school soccer team during the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Season should not be registered
for MLS NEXT Homegrown Division during that Fall, Winter or Spring high school season
period. If a Club wishes to add the Primary Player to the roster at the conclusion of that Player’s
high school soccer season, that will count toward the Club’s player adds and the Primary Player
may enter the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Season as of the next Semester. For example, a
Primary Player without an approved MLS NEXT Homegrown Division High School Waiver
whose high school soccer season occurs in the Fall may begin MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
participation only after MLS NEXT Fest. Clubs found to violate this provision may be subject to
discipline.
Future Players are permitted to participate simultaneously in both (1) MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division and (2) high school soccer.
13

d. College Camps
Players will be permitted to attend winter college camp, provided that the Coach has approved
attendance in advance. The following guidelines are in place for college camps:
i. The camp must be hosted by an NCAA institution in accordance with
NCAA regulations;
ii. The camp may be no longer than three (3) days in length;
iii. The Player may only attend one (1) camp per school per year; and
iv. The Player may not attend a camp if it falls during the Competitive Season
or is equal to or less than one (1) week prior to the start of the Competitive
Season.
Summer college camps that fall outside of the Season do not apply and Players may attend such
camps without needing to request a waiver.
No insurance coverage provided by PDev will cover any occurrences arising out of attendance in
Non-MLS NEXT Homegrown Division activities, including, but not limited to, winter or summer
college camps, tryouts, or other competitions.
VIII. EXPECTATIONS OF CLUBS
a. Training and Match Expectations
All Clubs are required to complete their entire Regular Season schedule and to attend all MLS
NEXT Homegrown Division Events scheduled for their age groups. As further detailed in the
Membership Agreement, failure to field a Team for a scheduled Match resulting in a Forfeit will
cause the Club to be fined. All Forfeits are subject to additional fines and disciplinary action at
PDev’s sole and absolute discretion. Any Club forfeiting a Match due to failing to field a Team
during its membership in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division may be removed from the Program
in PDev’s sole and absolute discretion.
Clubs are responsible for fielding teams that provide meaningful competition for other Members.
In the event a Club is not able to field consistently competitive teams in MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division, PDev will notify the Club of the concern and work in good faith on solutions and
timelines to improve the Club’s competitiveness. In the event the Club is unable to become more
competitive, such that the Club is providing meaningful Matches for other Members, the Club may
be subject to removal from the Program.
Clubs are responsible for adhering to all Match Day policies and procedures outlined and explained
in the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Match Day Guide. Clubs should make efforts to acquire
the best facilities possible as outlined below. Clubs must train and play home Matches within
reasonable geographic proximity to their Club’s home location unless approved by PDev. If a Club
is unable to provide a home field that meets MLS NEXT Homegrown Division minimum standards
14

(minimum of 70 x 110 yards for U15 and older; minimum of 64 x 100 yards for U14; and minimum
of 65 x 84 for U13), the Club may be asked to relocate to an alternate location or may lose the
right to host Matches.
b. Inclusion & Non-Discrimination
MLS NEXT Homegrown Division is an inclusive and non-discriminatory Program. PDev does
not and will not tolerate any racist, discriminatory or inappropriately exclusive behavior by any
Participant, including any Club.
PDev has set out to create an equitable and inclusive development environment. To that end, Clubs
shall make all efforts to establish a positive and safe environment for all Participants, without
regard to race, ethnicity, income level, culture, sexual orientation, and gender. As outlined in the
MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy, it is recommended that each Club
provide inclusion and awareness training for its Participants. To promote a positive and inclusive
culture, Clubs should use best efforts to provide training on an annual basis and ensure that their
Players and Club Representatives complete such training prior to the beginning of the Regular
Season (and more frequently if needed).
c. Non-Technical Facility Requirements
Clubs should acquire Facilities that (if feasible): (1) have separate locker rooms for Players on
different teams; (2) have separate and functionally equivalent changing areas for different genders,
as well as separate restrooms for Adults and Minors; and (3) the field must be designed for soccer
use and adequately maintained and repaired. In addition, prior to the use of a field, the field must
be inspected as set forth in the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy.
d. Mandatory Meeting Attendance
The Club must send at least one (1) Club director and one (1) Club administrator to each mandatory
MLS NEXT Homegrown Division meeting or conference call as scheduled by PDev, unless
otherwise notified.
e. Recruiting Players/Territories
No Club has exclusive territory rights. Clubs may recruit Players for participation on Clubs from
any geographic region. Players cannot move back and forth between Clubs more than once in a
given Season. Please refer to the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Player Movement Guidelines
with respect to requests for trials and player transfers, and related communications. MLS
Academies must abide by the MLS Player Development Guidelines.
IX. CLUB FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITIES
a. Performance Bond
As further described in the Membership Agreement, each Club will be required to post a
performance bond of $5,000 prior to being declared an official Member. In the event PDev does
15

not approve the Club for renewed membership, PDev will return the Club’s performance bond. In
the event a Club is removed from MLS NEXT Homegrown Division for any reason outlined in
the Rules and Policies, the performance bond shall be forfeited by the Club and the Club shall have
no further rights regarding the performance bond.
b. Travel Costs
All costs associated with travel to MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competitions are the
responsibility of the individual Club.
c. Training/Coaching Costs
All costs associated with the training and coaching of a Club are the responsibility of the individual
Club.
d. Administrative Costs
All costs associated with running a Club, including, without limitation, all insurance premiums
and deductibles required by the Rules and Policies or otherwise, are the responsibility of the
individual Club.
e. Registration Costs
All Players and Club Representatives who will be allowed on any Team’s technical area during a
Match must register with PDev to participate in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division. As detailed in
the Membership Agreement, registration fees for the upcoming season shall be $60 per
participating individual. Coaches and Club Representatives do not need to pay more if coaching
across different age groups or serving in multiple roles, each individual only needs to be accounted
for once. Fees are subject to change in PDev’s sole and absolute discretion upon reasonable notice
to Clubs. Clubs must pay registration fees for all players and staff members before PDev will
approve them for participation in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division.
f. Equipment Costs
Other than match balls and any other equipment that PDev elects to provide in its sole discretion,
all costs associated with equipment are the responsibility of the individual Club.
g. Facility Costs
All costs associated with securing a Facility for training and home Matches are the responsibility
of the individual Club. The host Club is responsible for covering Facility parking fees (if
applicable) for the Match Officials, visiting Club Representatives, and Team transportation (e.g.,
bus/van) for Matches.
16

h. Match Officials Fees
The home Team will cover referee, assistant referee, and assignor fees for all age groups in
Matches during the Regular Season that are scheduled by PDev. The home and visiting Teams will
split previously stated referee fees for all age groups at Matches that are not part of the Regular
Season, including the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Showcases, MLS NEXT Cup, and any
other MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Event requiring Match Officials. PDev reserves the right
to assess sanctions for outstanding Match Official fees.
i. Fines/Penalties
PDev reserves the right to assess fines and/or sanctions for Infractions, as outlined in the MLS
NEXT Homegrown Division Disciplinary Code. All of the money collected in fines will be
provided to the MLS GO PLAY FUND, Major League Soccer’s initiative to make soccer more
accessible for all children.
j. Certificate of Insurance
Each Club shall carry and maintain liability insurance and, upon request by PDev and at Club’s
insurance renewals, shall provide PDev with certificate(s) of insurance evidencing the Club’s
insurance coverage in the amounts and for the coverages required by PDev and the Rules and
Policies. For further information, see the Membership Agreement.
k. Other
Unless otherwise expressly addressed in these regulations, all expenses related to a Club and the
Club’s participation in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division are the sole responsibility of the
participating Club.
X. FINANCIAL SUPPORT PROVIDED BY PDEV
a. Secondary Player Medical Insurance
PDev provides secondary medical accident insurance for each Player. This medical accident
insurance is secondary to (i.e., excess over) the Player’s personal health insurance and only covers
injuries occurring during Matches, MLS NEXT Homegrown Division training, MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division tryouts, and MLS NEXT Homegrown Division tournaments. For the
avoidance of doubt, this medical insurance does not cover Coaches, other staff, or spectators.
Further, PDev will not cover health insurance for Players that drop their primary health insurance
after joining PDev for the purpose of making this insurance the Player’s primary medical insurance
policy. Sublimits, deductibles, coinsurance, and other conditions may apply.
b. Background Check Costs
PDev will cover all costs associated with the background checks required in the Rules and Policies
and conducted through the PDev Registration system by PDev’s selected vendor.
17

c. ImPACT Baseline Tests
PDev will cover all costs associated with the administration and delivery of individual ImPACT
baseline tests that are required for all Players on an annual basis.
XI. REGISTRATION
a. General
PDev will collect the personal information of all Players and certain Club Representatives via its
online registration system software, to the extent such information is necessary to enable PDev to
provide services (e.g., match execution).
PDev shall have the authority to establish additional procedures, including deadlines, for
Registration of Players through the registration software.
b. Club and Club Representative Registration
i. Club Registration
All Clubs must register through the online Registration process via the registration platform
designated by PDev, which shall include the following:
• Registration of Club in PDev’s designated soccer registration software; and
• Paying a $60 registration fee for each individual that will be on the field or sideline for a
Club during a Match.
• Individuals who have not cleared the background check process or received approval from
PDev or its designated third-party vendor cannot participate in any MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division activity.
• Any Club that allows an unapproved/unregistered individual to participate in any MLS
NEXT Homegrown Division activity may be subject to Sanctions including, but not limited
to, a fine or termination of its Membership, and will be considered in breach of its
Membership Agreement.
ii. Club Representative Registration
All Club Representatives seeking to work with any Player, including, without limitation, any
Covered Person as defined in the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy,
registered Health Care Professionals (“HCP”), Certified Athletic Trainers (“ATC”) at any practice,
Match, or other MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Event must be fully registered. Names and
photos are created digitally through the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division registration software
and will be used to check in Club Representatives who will be in the Club’s technical area via the
Match Day Roster. A maximum of five (5) Coaches/staff members (not including Qualified
Medical Professional (“QMP”) or other HCP/ATC, video analyst, videographer, Club Sporting
18

Director, or Club General Manager) from a Club may be in the technical area during a Match and
all must have a photo within the Match Day Roster.
To request approval, all Club Representatives must complete the online Registration process
through PDev’s platform by:
• Completing the PDev-mandated background check (and for new hires a reference check
conducted by the Club as well);
• Uploading a photo of themselves;
• Completing the U.S. Center for SafeSport’s Core Training1 (“Core Training”) (for initial
approval) or, if the Core training has already been completed, the official SafeSport
refresher course for each subsequent season (available in your training account at
www.safesport.org);
• Acknowledge all provided Rules and Policies;
• Completing the CDC HEADS UP concussion training; and
• Submitting a signed MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Participant Agreement and Waiver
for the applicable Season.
NOTE: Individuals subject to disciplinary sanction by the Center or PDev, whether a temporary
or final measure, may not be permitted to participate in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division. Clubs
must consult the on-line disciplinary records at www.safesport.org
(https://uscenterforsafesport.org/response-and-resolution/centralized-disciplinary-database/) as
part of their employment background screening process.
All Coaches and Club directors are required to complete annual equity and inclusion training, as
directed by PDev.
c. Player Registration
All individuals seeking to play for an MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Club must be fully
registered. Names and photos are created digitally through the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
registration software and will be used to check in Players who will be in the Club’s technical area
via the Match Day Roster.
1 Pursuant to the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy, PDev will make its SafeSport
training code available for the use of any PDev affiliate (including all clubs with which Clubs are affiliated), upon
request and at no additional cost to the Club or the non-MLS NEXT entity to which it is being provided. Contact
PDev at mlsnext@mlsplayerdevelopment.com for more details.
19

All Players must complete the online registration process through PDev via the designated
registration platform, including the following:
i. Execute the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Participant Agreement and
Waiver and the Player Authorization for the Release of Health Information;
ii. Complete an ImPACT Baseline Test;
iii. Upload a photo;
iv. Provide medical insurance information;
v. Provide required medical clearance information;
vi. Acknowledge and agree to comply with the MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division Code of Conduct, MLS Privacy Policy, the MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Rules and Regulations and the MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy;
vii. Complete all aspects of the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Registration
process and affirm you are not subject to suspension; and
viii. Complete mandated SafeSport training, if applicable.
d. Compliance with the Registration Process
i. PDev may disapprove, deny registration to, and/or disqualify any
individual, and/or deny Membership to or otherwise discipline any Club,
which employs or utilizes any individual who either fails to comply with
the registration procedures, falsifies information, violates the Rules and
Policies, or as a result of such procedures is determined by PDev to be unfit
to continue in their current position.
ii. Anyone who forges a document or falsifies an authentic document or uses
a forged or falsified document to deceive may be Sanctioned.
XII. PLAYER ELIGIBILITY AND ROSTERING
a. Eligible Players
To be considered an eligible Player, any Player listed on any Club Roster must meet the age
requirement for their Club, have completed the registration process outlined in Section XI(c), have
paid the registration fee, and must not be subject to a Suspension (to serve a suspension, a Player
is required to sit for the entire subsequent match day; the Player is not eligible to play with an older
age group on the day the Player serves the suspension).
Players can only be approved and made eligible by PDev.
20

Players not listed on a Club’s Roster or any Player listed on a Club’s Roster but not eligible to
play, due to reasons such as missing MLS NEXT Homegrown Division registration forms,
awaiting international clearance, serving a suspension, or not being listed on the official Match
Day Roster and line-up sheet, shall be considered an ineligible Player. A Club using an ineligible
Player during an MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competition may be subject to a Sanction as
determined by PDev and/or a Forfeit (by a 0-3 margin) of any Matches in which the ineligible
Player participated.
Each participating Club shall ensure the following when selecting its Players for each age group
in an MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competition:
i. All Players remain subject to PDev jurisdiction;
ii. All Players shall be eligible for participation in accordance with the relevant
MLS NEXT Homegrown Division and FIFA regulations; and
iii. All Players shall meet the applicable age requirements:
Age Group Born on or After
U13 January 1, 2013
U14 January 1, 2012
U15 January 1, 2011
U16 January 1, 2010
U17 January 1, 2009
U19 January 1, 2007
b. MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Club Roster
i. Match Day Rosters:
Each Team must create and submit via a digital form a Match Day Roster to the referee prior to a
Match. Each Match Day Roster must include all Players and Match Day Club Representatives.
Clubs are responsible for verifying the accuracy of Match Day Rosters and signing a completed
copy from the Match Officials after the match. PDev will update the Rosters weekly on the MLS
NEXT Homegrown Division Registration website to reflect any suspensions, additions or drops,
or other eligibility issues.
ii. General:
The date by which each Club must provide an initial MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Roster for
the 2025–2026 season is August 22, 2025. Each Club’s Roster may have a maximum of twenty-
21

eight (28) Players per age group and a minimum of 14 Primary Players per age group. There is no
limit to the number of Rostered Players that do not have United States citizenship.
iii. Primary Player Adds:
Clubs will have one period during the season (August 22, 2025, through April 16, 2026) at which
time Players may be added as Primary Player (either adding a new Primary Player or moving a
Future Player to Primary Player status).
The Club will have a total of six (6) Primary Player Adds per MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
Team with the discretion to allocate the Player Adds across their MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division Teams as they see fit. The total number of Primary Player adds should not exceed the
number of MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Teams multiplied by six (6) and can only be utilized
during this window. For example, an MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Club with only U15 and
U17 teams can add up to, but not exceed, twelve (12) total Players across the two teams.
Clubs that pass a Player on to a Club with an MLS professional pathway will not be charged with
using a Player Add to replace that individual. In addition, Clubs that promote a Player on to an
MLS professional team in that Club’s pathway in good faith will not be charged with using a Player
Add to replace that individual and will be permitted an extra roster spot in the event that the
promoted Player returns to the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Team.
Any unused Player Adds cannot be utilized beyond the deadline unless a Club is promoting
a Future Player (added before the deadline) to Primary Player after the Player Add window and
has an open Roster slot.
iv. Injured Players:
If a Club is operating at the Roster maximum, it may replace a Player with a serious injury (as
supported by written documentation from a medical professional detailing the injury) with a new
Player in their Roster spot. If the injured Player is medically cleared to return during the Season,
the Club may allow him/her/them to replace the Player that took his/her/their Roster spot with no
deduction of Player Adds.
v. Player Movement:
Please refer to MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Player Movement Guidelines.
vi. Future Players:
Clubs may add Future Players across all of a Club’s age groups (U13 through U19), which will
count against Roster totals, but will not count as a Primary Add.
A Future Player is a Player who is Rostered with a Club who is not ready to contribute at the MLS
NEXT Homegrown Division level full time. A Future Player is not playing consistently and/or
playing enough minutes in Matches to fully develop; therefore, the Player is eligible to play
matches outside of MLS NEXT Homegrown Division with the Club with which they are
22

registered. A Future Player is required to train a minimum of 1x per week with the MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division Team that registered them as a Future Player and overall should consistently
train 3-4 times per week (depending on the age group).
A Future Player can play in a maximum of seven (7) Matches in a Season. Note, at U13 and U14
age groups, any Future Player added to the Match Day Roster will count as having played in a
Match. To supplement his/her/their Match minutes, a Future Player is eligible to also play matches
outside MLS NEXT Homegrown Division with a non-MLS NEXT Homegrown Division team
within the Club as part of his/her/their individual development plan. A Future Player must still
follow the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division play-to-rest ratio.
A Future Player may not be registered with two different Clubs at the same time during the same
Season. A Future Player may only change his/her/their status to Primary once during the Season
and may not return to Future Player status in the same Season. Furthermore, a Future Player does
not automatically garner Primary Player status after participating in his/her/their seventh (7th)
Match. The Club must complete a Designation Change to move the Future Player to Primary Player
status. Players who have played in the maximum seven (7) Matches are not eligible to continue to
play in Matches unless the Club indicates the desire to move the Player to Primary Player status
and the Player status has been updated to Primary Player. Continued usage of a Future Player after
his/her/their seventh (7th) Match may cause a Club to forfeit Matches due to the use of an ineligible
Player. In addition, Future Players are eligible to participate in all MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division Events, without the Event Matches counting towards the seven (7) Match total.
vii. Match Roster Size:
For U13 through U19 Matches, only eighteen (18) Players from the Roster will be eligible to
participate in each Match, and these Players must be placed on the Match Day Roster (showing
Players that will start and Players eligible for substitution).
viii. Bench and Technical Area:
The bench and technical area are reserved for Players and Club Representatives listed on the
Team’s Match Day Roster (a maximum of eighteen (18) Players, including seven (7) subs on
bench). In addition, a maximum of five (5) registered Club Representatives may sit in the technical
area, not including a QMP or other registered HCP or ATC. Any person in the technical area
during a Match must be registered with PDev as a Player or Club Representative and listed on the
Match Day Roster, other than a QMP or other HCP or ATC that is not employed by the Club. Any
person in the technical area during a Match who is not registered with PDev as a Player or Club
Representative will be subject to Sanctions.
c. Younger Players Playing Up
Players on the U13, U14, U15, U16 and U17 MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Teams may be
selected for the U14, U15, U16, U17 and U19 Match Day Rosters, respectively. The 25% start
recommendation and 50% minutes played for U13 age group is inclusive of all Matches started in
23

any MLS NEXT Homegrown Division age group. Players playing up can only compete in
cumulative minutes equaling one full match (i.e., 90 minutes for U17 and U19 Players) per day
split across two (2) Matches.
d. Late Registration
Any registration requests received after Thursday at 5:00 pm Eastern Time for the upcoming
weekend Fixtures may not be approved in time for the upcoming Fixture. Further, any registration
requests are subject to PDev’s approval in its sole discretion.
e. International Clearance
Per FIFA’s Regulations on the Status and Transfer of Players, as it may be amended or restated
from time to time, the international clearance process is required when any foreign-born Player
over the age of 10 attempts to register with an affiliated club in the United States, regardless of
that Player’s ability or citizenship.2 FIFA regulations require PDev to obtain appropriate
clearances on behalf of these Players before they can register and participate in MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division.
XIII. COMPETITION
a. Overview
PDev will place Clubs into geographically-based Conferences and set a schedule. PDev reserves
the right to adjust Match scheduling.
b. Schedule
PDev will create and manage the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division competition schedule,
inclusive of the Post-Season. The MLS NEXT Cup typically will be held at the end of the Regular
2 According to FIFA, these Regulations serve an important function in protecting minors: “Young footballers are
vulnerable to potential exploitation and abuse when they are in a foreign country without proper controls. While
international transfers might, in specific cases, be favourable to a young player’s sporting career, they are likely
to be contrary to the best interests of the vast majority of players as minors. For FIFA, protecting the appropriate
and stable development of a minor as a whole should prevail over purely sporting interests. In the past, the
trafficking of young players to clubs, mainly in Europe, by unscrupulous persons led to some minors, whose talent
may not have met the expectations of the respective clubs, being virtually abandoned on the streets in foreign
countries. In these cases, minor players often do not know the language or culture and have no means to return to
their home countries. It is also necessary to mention that, in some cases, what is questioned is not necessarily the
quality of the training and overall education provided to the minors by this or that club, but the methods used to
bring the minors into the club. Today, although FIFA’s approach may appear harsh in an individual case, it is
only by enforcing the rules consistently and strictly that the abuses of the past can be avoided, and a proper and
safe development of minor players secured.”
24

Season. If a Club would like to change a Fixture date, it must contact PDev four (4) weeks in
advance of the Fixture date. All changes are subject to the approval of PDev.
c. Venue/Kick-off Times/Turnaround Time
PDev shall set the dates of all MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Competitions. The venues and
specific kick-off times of the Matches shall be proposed by the home Team, subject to the approval
of PDev. The home Team shall submit venues and specific kick-off times to PDev for all Matches
by a date determined each year by Program Staff, so visiting Teams can make the appropriate
arrangements or offer objections for PDev’s consideration. All Clubs must provide a turf or
synthetic alternative in the event of inclement weather. Evening Matches may only be played at
venues with appropriate lighting installations.
i. Double Fixture weekends may be played; however, a minimum of eighteen
(18) hours must be provided between both kick-off times to allow for
necessary travel and recovery.
1. For instance, if a Saturday Fixture kick-off is 6:00 pm local time,
the Sunday Fixture may not kick-off earlier than 12:00 pm local time
unless authorized by PDev.
ii. Clubs may not request to schedule three (3) Matches in four (4) days during
Regular Season competition.
iii. Matches may not kick off earlier than 9:00 am local time without permission
from Program Staff.
iv. When scheduling home Matches, Clubs should adhere to the following
guidelines with regard to kick-off times and order of Matches:
1. The home Team determines kick-off order.
2. PDev recommends that Matches be played from oldest to youngest
when possible.
3. When scheduling multiple Matches on the same field, please allow
for at least thirty (30) minutes of warm-up time prior to each kickoff.
4. Less than thirty (30) minutes between each match is permitted if
there is adequate warm-up space on an adjacent field.
v. Moving a Fixture venue or kickoff time for any reason within fourteen (14)
days of the scheduled Fixture may result in fines and/or Sanctions that will
be levied in accordance with the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
Disciplinary Code.
25

d. Facility Set-Up
The home Team shall be responsible for setting up its venue to include the appropriate lines, goals,
nets, corner flags, benches, and water set up for both Teams and ensuring a clean and well-
maintained field in accordance with the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing
Policy. The home Team will also provide the official adidas match balls provided by PDev.
e. Abandonment
If a Club refuses to play a Match or to continue playing one that has begun, and there is no rationale
under the Rules and Policies detailing why this action would be permissible, it may be Sanctioned
by forfeiting the Match. Additional Sanctions may be imposed against the Club in PDev’s sole
discretion. In serious cases, the Club may also be disqualified from the MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division Competition in progress and its Membership may be revoked.
f. Postponement
All postponements and cancellations for any reason other than the health and safety of Participants
must be approved by PDev. A Club may postpone any Match in the event the Club believes in
good faith that proceeding with a Match would pose a significant risk to the health and safety of
any Participants.
g. Rescheduling a Postponed Match
Provided the postponement is approved by PDev or is made in accordance with the exceptions
provided for herein, the Clubs in question are given the first opportunity to arrange a reschedule
date for a postponed Match. If there is no rescheduled date within seven (7) days after the
postponed Fixture, PDev will choose the date, location, and time for the rescheduled Match and
each Club will be required to participate, subject to any health and safety guidelines released by
PDev. Fees associated with that date will be the responsibility of the involved Club(s).
h. Rescheduling Considerations
U13 Matches must reach the 50th minute to be considered a completed Match. U14 Matches must
reach the 60th minute to be considered a completed Match. U15, U16, U17, and U19 Matches must
reach the 75th minute to be considered a completed Match. If a Match does not reach the designated
threshold, PDev at its sole discretion shall have the authority to review all of the facts (including
fault on the part of either Team, unavoidable conditions, expense for both Teams, and requests by
a Team to take a particular action in the best interests of MLS NEXT Homegrown Division, the
Clubs, and the Club’s teams) in determining whether and when a Match should be rescheduled,
who should bear the financial burden as a result of cancellation or rescheduling, whether the Match
should be forfeited or cancelled permanently, or whether other fines should be levied. Unless the
Sanction or remedy is set forth specifically in any Rules and Policies, PDev shall decide what
action to take, and PDev’s decision shall be final (subject to the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
Disciplinary Code).
26

i. Schedule Completion
Every Team must play every Match on its schedule. Teams that fail to play every Match may face
a Forfeit or may be subject to a fine. Further, PDev, at its sole discretion, may exclude any
Club/Team from Post-Season participation for failure to play every scheduled Match.
j. Facility Clearances
Once a Club has submitted its venues and kick-off times, PDev assumes that a Team’s home
Facility is available for all dates on its schedule. If the home Facility is not available, an alternative
location must be provided by the home Team. For the avoidance of doubt, all facilities remain
subject to approval by PDev and must comply with all Rules and Policies. PDev reserves the right
to reject approval for any facility at PDev’s sole and reasonable discretion.
k. Standings
PDev assumes the responsibility of posting the official MLS NEXT Homegrown Division league
standings on the MLS NEXT Website for the U15 – U19 ages. The official MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division league standings include wins, losses, ties, goals scored for, goals scored
against, points per match, and total league points.
Result Points
Win 3
Tie 1
Loss 0
Forfeit 0–3 loss
All standings are generated by the Match Report submitted online by the referee within twenty-
four (24) hours of the completion of the Match. Teams have forty-eight (48) hours to verify their
Match Results with PDev.
l. Conference Tiebreakers
If two (2) or more Clubs finish the Regular Season with an equal number of Points per Match the
following procedure (in order of priority) will be used to break the tie and determine the final
standings (“Regular Season Tiebreaker”):
i. Head-to-head (only applicable to tie of two (2) Clubs).
ii. Number of Wins per Match.
iii. Goal differential per Match.
iv. Goals for per Match.
27

v. Away goal differential per Match
vi. Away goals for per Match
vii. Home goal differential per Match
viii. Home goals for per Match
ix. Fewest Disciplinary points:
1. One (1) point will be assessed for every Yellow Card received
during the competition.
2. Three (3) points will be assessed for every Red Card received during
the competition.
3. Five (5) points will be assessed for any ejections of staff or Players
from the technical area during the competition.
x. Coin toss (tie of two (2) Clubs) or drawing of lots (tie of three (3) or more
Clubs).
After the winner of the Regular Season Tiebreaker advances, any remaining tied Clubs who did
not advance from the first round Regular Season Tiebreaker shall go through a second round
Regular Season Tiebreaker in order to determine the next advancing Club. The Regular Season
Tiebreaker system will continue until qualification/seeding has been determined.
m. Wildcard Tiebreakers
Wildcard tiebreakers shall be determined in the same manner as Conference tiebreakers.
XIV. MATCHES, LAWS OF THE GAME, DISCIPLINARY MATTERS,
DISPUTES, PROTESTS, AND APPEALS
a. Laws of the Game
Unless otherwise provided in the Rules and Policies, all Matches shall be played in accordance
with the Laws of the Game in force at the time of the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
Competition and as promulgated by the International Football Association Board, which can be
found here.3 In the case of any discrepancy in the interpretation of the Laws of the Game, the
English version shall be authoritative.
3 https://www.theifab.com/downloads/laws-of-the-game-2025-26?l=en
28

b. Disciplinary Matters
• Incidents and events requiring disciplinary action are handled in compliance with the
MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Disciplinary Code. The Players and Clubs
participating in MLS NEXT Homegrown Division agree to comply with the Laws of the
Game (as described above) and all Rules and Policies. The Players, Coaches, Club
Representatives, and Clubs agree to comply with all further directives set forth by PDev
from time to time, including, without limitation, any health and safety requirements or
protocols.
• Any Coach/Club Representative who has received a Match Suspension cannot be present
at the Club facilities (including the soccer fields or Club offices) and cannot attend any
Club-approved activity including, but not limited to, a Match, MLS NEXT Homegrown
Division Tournament, or other MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Event during the
pendency of the Sanction. Any suspended Coach/Club Representative who is found to
have violated this prohibition will receive an additional Match Suspension of two (2)
Fixtures.
• Reporting Egregious Fouls After the Fact
a. Clubs have 72 hours following a Match to report any egregious in-match fouls, not
acknowledged by the referee, to PDev after noticing the egregious foul during film
review.
c. Protests of Officiating Decisions
If a Match Decision is subject to Protest, it shall be submitted to the jurisdiction of PDev.
i. Time: Unless otherwise stipulated in this article, Protests shall be submitted
in writing to PDev within twelve (12) hours of the conclusion of the Match
in question and followed up immediately with a full written report,
including a copy of the original Protest, to be sent in writing to PDev;
otherwise, they will be disregarded.
ii. Eligibility: Protests regarding the eligibility of Matches shall be submitted
in writing to PDev no later than 24 hours after the Match.
iii. Field Conditions: Protests regarding the state of the field, its surroundings,
markings, or accessory items (e.g., goals, flag posts, or soccer balls) shall
be made to the referee before the start of the Match by the Coach or
administrator lodging the Protest and should be included in the referee’s
post-Match Report. PDev must be notified in writing of the Protest prior to
kick-off. If the field’s playing surface becomes unplayable during a Match,
the referee shall consult PDev and both parties shall have the joint right to
determine whether to delay, postpone, or cancel the Match in compliance
with the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Safety and Wellbeing Policy.
29

iv. After a Match: Protests against any incidents that occur during the course
of a Match shall be made to PDev immediately following the completion of
the Match.
v. Referee’s Decisions: No Protests may be made about the referee’s decisions
regarding facts connected with play; such decisions are final.
vi. Frivolous Protests: If an unfounded or irresponsible Protest is lodged, PDev
may dismiss the Protest.
vii. For the avoidance of doubt, all violations of Rules and Policies other than
Match Decisions remain subject to the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
Disciplinary Code.
d. Match Integrity Policy
PDev prohibits match manipulation of any kind. Clubs that condone or contemplate match
manipulation will be subject to immediate expulsion from MLS NEXT Homegrown Division and
may face additional Sanctions.
PDev applies the FIFA Code of Ethics and prohibits involvement in soccer sports betting by any
Club Representatives or Players, regardless of whether sports betting may be legal in such
jurisdiction.
e. Disputes
Any Participant, Club, Club Representative, or individual or entity affiliated with any Club that is
involved in a dispute with PDev or a Club must first make a good faith effort to resolve such
dispute through negotiation before taking any other action. All disputes arising out of or related to
the Membership Agreement or the MLS NEXT Homegrown Division Participant Agreement and
Waiver shall be resolved by arbitration in accordance with the terms in those agreements.
XV. COMMERCIAL RIGHTS AND EQUIPMENT
All Participants acknowledge that MLS is the owner of all MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
marks and that use of said marks is limited to what is expressly permitted and limited to the term
during which the particular Participant is affiliated with a Member or is itself a Club.
a. Uniforms
i. Players, Coaches, and Club Representatives are not allowed to display any
image or writing in any language or form on their playing or Team
uniforms, equipment (including bags, beverage containers, medical bags,
etc.), or body for the duration of official Matches, except for inoffensive
Team names and logos, sponsorships, and Player names, which are all
subject to review by PDev in advance of being displayed during Matches.
Players, Coaches, and Club Representatives with offensive, obscene,
30

distracting, or age -inappropriate tattoos may be asked to cover them for the
duration of a Match. Clubs are required to have one white (or similarly
light-colored) uniform and one dark uniform. Home Teams will always
wear white/light -color uniforms and visiting Teams will be required to wear
their dark/contrasting uniforms on single-Fixture weekends. In the event a
Club has a double Fixture weekend, please refer to the Match Day Guide
for the uniform protocol. Clubs are required to apply the MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division uniform patch on the left sleeve of their match
uniform. In the event the Club cannot reasonably apply the patch on the left
sleeve, the Club must contact PDev for guidance on an alternate location.
In the event Clubs are not provided an MLS NEXT Homegrown Division
patch for the beginning of the 2025–2026 Season, Clubs must cover up the
U.S. Soccer Development Academy (or other national league) patch on their
match uniform. MLS NEXT Homegrown Division uniform patches will be
supplied to the Club at no cost. However, the Club is responsible for any
costs associated with the application of its patch to its uniforms.
b. Soccer Balls
i. All Matches are to be played with the official Match ball. PDev will provide
a set number of Match balls at no cost to the Clubs. No Match can be played
without properly inflated Match balls. There shall be a minimum of four (4)
Match balls at each match which shall be inspected and subject to approval
by the referee. The U13 age group will play with size 4 balls. All other age
groups will play with size 5 balls, filled to 9.0–10.5 psi.
c. Commercial Rights
Clubs shall have the right to commercialize certain items in connection with their MLS NEXT
Homegrown Division participation, as outlined in the MLS NEXT Commercial Marketing
Guidelines. Any Club that wishes to obtain sponsors in connection with its Club must ensure that
all such sponsorships comply with the MLS NEXT Commercial Marketing Guidelines. All
decisions regarding the MLS NEXT Commercial Marketing Guidelines and any interpretation of
the provisions of the guidelines shall be at the sole and absolute discretion of PDev.
XVI. REFEREE AND MATCH INFORMATION
a. Overview
The referees, assistant referees, and fourth officials (when applicable) will be assigned for each
Match by the Referee Management Group determined in PDev’s sole discretion.
b. Fees
Referee fees will be paid as set forth in Section IX(h).
31

c. Match Report
After each Match, the referee shall complete the official Match Report form. The referee shall
enter the game data using the online reporting system designated by PDev within 24 hours of the
Match. The Clubs will be responsible for reviewing the online Match Report and submitting
corrections to the appropriate Member in a timely matter. The Match Report will be used to
generate standings, track suspensions, and record statistics.
d. Match Information
On the Match Report, the referee shall note all occurrences of significance, such as injury (serious,
any suspected head injury, or any injury that requires emergency medical attention), misconduct
of Players leading to caution or expulsion, unsporting behavior by supporters and/or by Club
Representatives, or any other person acting on behalf of a Club at the Match and any other incident
happening before, during, and after the Match in as much detail as possible.
XVII. MISCELLANEOUS
a. Special Circumstances
PDev, in its sole and absolute discretion, will issue any instructions necessitated by special
circumstances that may arise. These instructions shall form an integral part of these Rules and
Regulations and may, in exceptional circumstances, override a specific regulation.
b. Matters Not Provided For
Matters not provided for in these Rules and Regulations, and cases of force majeure shall be
decided by the MLS NEXT General Manager. All decisions will be final.
c. Audit of Club
PDev reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to audit a Club to ensure compliance with any and
all Rules and Policies.
32
